mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index transmits positive earnings momentum to the S&P 500, generating positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, elevated joblessness signals weak household income growth and suppressed consumer demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is above 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increase in consumer prices while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0% generates negative real interest rates that divert capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Within high-unemployment regimes, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal a demand-led recovery that outperforms defensive expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, month-over-month increases in HOUST coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the federal funds rate triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing toward equities as discount rates fall, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month decreases in FEDFUNDS coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates contracting consumer demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month decreases in RSAFS coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls signal a deteriorating labor market that compresses consumption and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following two consecutive monthly declines in PAYEMS, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP indicates contracting aggregate demand that reduces earnings visibility and widens risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter declines in GDPC1 coincide with positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations.
